cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the question carefully. The question is asking why the text uses a lot of figures (numbers). The options are: (A) record the valuable data (B) provide concrete evidence (C) introduce a research method (D) give a detailed description First, I need to understand what the text is doing with the figures. Let me look through the text again. The text discusses the growth of credit card ownership in China. It provides several percentages and growth rates, such as: - "about 30 percent of Chinese urban households now own at least one credit card" - "growth rate of credit card adoption has been an average of 40 percent per year between 2004 and 2009" - "more than 90 percent of non-credit card users were unaware of safety issues" - "more than 60 percent had little knowledge of the consequences of credit card debt" - "72 percent of Chinese credit card holders were never late on their payments" - "only 55 percent were able to pay the full balance" - "70 percent of Chinese credit card holders said that credit cards contributed to overspending" These figures are being used by the researcher (Rui Yao) to support points about the implications of credit card usage in China, both positive and negative. The researcher is making an argument about the effects of credit card adoption and knowledge gaps among users. Now, let's look at the options: (A) record the valuable data - This suggests the figures are just being stored or documented for future use, but the text is using them to make a point or support an argument. (B) provide concrete evidence - This suggests the figures are being used to support the claims or arguments being made in the text, which seems to be the case here. The researcher is using these statistics to support their analysis of the situation with credit cards in China. (C) introduce a research method - This would suggest the text is explaining how the research was conducted (e.g., sample size, methodology), but I don't see any details about how the data was collected or the research methods used. (D) give a detailed description - This could be possible because the text is describing the situation with numbers, but "detailed description" seems more about elaborating on a topic rather than using numbers to support a point.
